name: PyGetWindow (Cross-Platform Window Management)

performance.notes: System call overhead for window queries; fast enough for automation scripts

agent_readiness.notes: Window manipulation for Python desktop automation. import pygetwindow as gw. windows = gw.getWindowsWithTitle('Notepad'). win = windows[0]. win.activate(). win.move(100, 200). win.resize(800, 600). win.minimize(); win.maximize(); win.restore(). win.close(). win.left, win.top, win.width, win.height. gw.getActiveWindow(). gw.getAllWindows(). gw.getAllTitles(). macOS/Windows support; Linux support limited. Companion to pyautogui. Stalled development (last release 2020). PyWinCtl is a maintained fork/alternative.

gotchas: Development stalled since 2020 — last PyPI release is 0.0.9
macOS support limited compared to Windows
PyWinCtl is the maintained fork with better multi-platform support
best_when: Windows desktop automation needing window position/size control
Companion to pyautogui for full desktop automation
Simple window management in test automation scripts
When PyWinCtl's extra dependency weight is undesirable
avoid_when: Production automation (use PyWinCtl for maintained alternative)
Complex window management — use platform-native APIs
summary: PyGetWindow manages desktop windows from Python. import pygetwindow as gw; wins = gw.getWindowsWithTitle('App'); win.activate(); win.move(x, y); win.resize(w, h); win.minimize(); win.maximize(). BSD-3 by Al Sweigart. Stalled since 2020 — PyWinCtl is the maintained fork. Best for Windows desktop automation. Limited Linux support. Use with pyautogui for full desktop control.
